question: Is the NuScale SG tube material selection (Alloy 690) adequate for the expected service conditions?

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their protective claddings, and their compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It cites guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as relevant ASME code cases for materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures for pressurized water reactors.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and comply with relevant U.S. regulatory requirements to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance. These materials are subject to rigorous testing and quality assurance measures, with specific material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, with the RPV forming the SG shell and pressure boundary, and the SG tubes and welds contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV) is a key component of the reactor coolant system, housing the core, steam generators, and pressurizer, and serving as a barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its appurtenances are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) leakage detection system does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment with current instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until the source is located, and leakage rates are determined by RCS inventory balance; the detection systems are reliable and support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale Reactor Coolant System piping, specifically focusing on steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the primary degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that the NuScale design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear remaining the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and supports key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various system elements, and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance across its operational power range. It outlines the criteria and parameters used to optimize plant performance, such as coolant temperatures and flow rates, and explains that calculations are performed using thermal hydraulic software and verified manually. The analysis also accounts for uncertainties in flow resistance and design requirements, emphasizing the plant’s low pressure losses and natural circulation design.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale’s compliance with NRC regulations and ASME codes for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details the use of specific ASME BPVC and OM code cases for design, fabrication, inspection, and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet regulatory requirements and conditions as specified in relevant NRC regulations and regulatory guides. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific code cases applied.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III standards, with specific classifications for different piping systems, and references the applicable ASME BPVC edition and regulatory guides used for certification.

edges.description: Section A describes the materials used in the pressurizer and references where material specifications are detailed, while Section B cites industry guidelines and research reports that establish the adequacy and reliability of Alloy 690 for resistance to primary water stress corrosion cracking, thus providing the regulatory and technical basis for evaluating the material selection mentioned in Section A.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions, while Section B specifies that all RCPB materials (including those in Section A) must comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, thus connecting the specific pressurizer material details to the general regulatory compliance framework.

Section A references the locations where material specifications for pressurizer components are described, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, thus Section A indirectly points to the context in which the steam generator material (Alloy 690) selection is relevant.
Section A references Section B for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, indicating that the material details relevant to the pressurizer components are specified in Section B.

Section A references industry standards and research (such as EPRI guidelines and ASME codes) that are cited in Section B to demonstrate that the selected RCPB materials, including Alloy 690, comply with regulatory and code requirements.

Section A provides references to industry guidelines and materials reliability programs that establish criteria for material selection, which Section B applies by describing the use of Alloy 690 in the steam generator design.

Section A provides references to industry guidelines and materials reliability programs that establish requirements and best practices for material selection, which Section B applies by describing the actual materials used in the NuScale reactor vessel and steam generators, thus connecting regulatory expectations to design implementation.

Section A establishes that all Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary materials, which include the steam generator tubes, comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, while Section B identifies the steam generators as part of the reactor coolant system, thus linking the material selection in A to the specific component in B.

Section A states that the materials used for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes the steam generator tubes, comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, while Section B describes the reactor vessel and its components, including the steam generators, thereby specifying that the material selection in Section A applies to the components described in Section B.

Section A describes the overall design and components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, while Section B provides details about the materials used for the reactor vessel, both referencing the steam generators as key components within the reactor pressure vessel.

Section B references Section 5.2 for material descriptions, which is the section where Section A is located, indicating that material information in Section A is foundational for the component details in Section B.

Section A discusses the detection of reactor coolant pressure boundary leakage, while Section B cites EPRI and ASME references that include guidelines and reliability data for materials such as Alloy 690, which are relevant to evaluating the adequacy of material selection for service conditions.

Section B specifies that the SG tube material (Alloy 690) conforms to ASME and regulatory requirements, which supports the adequacy of material selection in the context of service conditions discussed in Section A regarding leakage detection.

Section A discusses the observed degradation mechanisms of Alloy 690 SG tubing in service, while Section B cites EPRI and ASME references that provide technical and regulatory guidance on material reliability and quality assurance for Alloy 690 under primary water conditions, thus Section A's claims about material adequacy are supported by the standards and research referenced in Section B.

Section A discusses the observed performance and degradation mechanisms of Alloy 690 SG tubing in service, while Section B confirms that the selected materials, including those for SG tubes, comply with ASME and regulatory requirements, thus connecting the material's operational adequacy to its regulatory conformance.

Section A describes operational procedures and water chemistry conditions relevant to the SGs, while Section B references industry guidelines and research on Alloy 690's resistance to corrosion, connecting the material selection to established standards and evidence for adequacy.

Section B specifies that the SG tube material (Alloy 690) conforms to ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, which supports the adequacy of the material selection discussed in Section A for the expected service conditions.

Section A references Section 5.4 for further description of the steam generators (SGs), which is where Section B provides detailed information about component materials, establishing a cross-reference between the system evaluation and the component design details.

Section A discusses the system evaluation and performance criteria for the NuScale SGs, while Section B references industry guidelines and research (including EPRI's report on Alloy 690's resistance to stress corrosion cracking), providing technical standards that inform or support the adequacy of the material selection mentioned in Section A.

Section B specifies that the SG tube material (Alloy 690) conforms to ASME and regulatory requirements, which Section A relies on to evaluate whether the material selection is adequate for service conditions.

Section B provides operational experience and degradation mechanisms for Alloy 690 SG tubing, which supports the assessment of material adequacy in Section A's context of reactor coolant pressure boundary integrity and leakage detection.

Section A discusses the detection of reactor coolant pressure boundary leakage, which is relevant to evaluating whether the SG tube material (Alloy 690) in Section B is adequate, as effective leakage detection is necessary to ensure the integrity of the selected material under service conditions.

Section B describes the system evaluation criteria and operating parameters for the steam generators (SGs), which establish the service conditions that the SG tube material (Alloy 690, referenced in the analysis question) must withstand, while Section A discusses leakage detection capabilities relevant to assessing the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes the SG tubes.

Section A discusses the observed degradation mechanisms (mainly wear) for SG tubes made of Alloy 690 in operating PWRs, while Section B describes the operational procedures and water chemistry controls that affect the service environment of the SG tubes, thus providing context for evaluating the adequacy of Alloy 690 under expected service conditions.

Section A discusses the observed degradation mechanisms (wear) for SG tubes made of Alloy 690 in operating PWRs, while Section B outlines the system evaluation criteria, including establishing steady-state parameters for optimal SG performance, thus Section A provides evidence about material performance that supports the adequacy of the material selection discussed in the context of Section B's performance criteria.

Section A describes operational procedures and water chemistry management for the steam generators (SGs), while Section B discusses the evaluation of SG performance under various service conditions, connecting them through the operational and environmental parameters that the SG tube material (Alloy 690) must withstand.

Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer, which includes information about the RPV upper shell and upper head materials.

Section A references Section B by relying on the EPRI and ASME documents listed in Section B to support compliance with material selection and code requirements for the reactor coolant boundary, including Alloy 690.

Section A references the use of ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements for overpressure protection, while Section B specifies that the SG tube material (Alloy 690) conforms to these same ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, thus showing that the material selection satisfies the applicable codes and standards referenced in Section A.
Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which is detailed in Section A.

Section A describes the design objective to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B cites references (including EPRI and ASME documents) that provide technical justification and industry standards for material selection such as Alloy 690, thereby supporting the adequacy of the material for service conditions.

Section B specifies that the RCPB materials, including the SG tube material (Alloy 690), conform to ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, thereby supporting Section A's claim that the RCPB integrity is maintained for the plant's design life.
Section B references Section A for material descriptions, indicating that the material details for the pressurizer components in Section B are specified in Section A.

Section A establishes compliance with ASME BPVC requirements for reactor coolant pressure boundary materials, while Section B cites technical references (including EPRI and ASME documents) that provide supporting data and guidelines relevant to the adequacy and reliability of Alloy 690 under service conditions, thereby supporting the regulatory compliance claim in Section A.

Section A states that the NuScale design complies with 10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC Section III for Class 1 components, while Section B specifies that the selected RCPB materials, including Alloy 690, conform to the fabrication and material requirements of those same codes and regulations, thus providing detailed material compliance supporting the general regulatory compliance claim in Section A.

Section A describes the design objective to maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), while Section B specifies that this integrity is ensured by compliance with regulatory codes (10 CFR 50.55a and ASME BPVC), thus Section B provides the regulatory framework that Section A's design must satisfy.
